old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-bdd5","title":"Persist link tuple state across reboot","description":"Python Node constructs LinkLayer with a random epoch from only 128 values and seqnum zero without restoring persistent state. Rebooting under the same identity can reuse a previously signed (epoch, seqnum) tuple. Persist the next tuple plus exhaustion state atomically, or rotate the identity key before transmitting when state is unavailable; add reboot/recovery tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-19T00:01:25.322514Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-19T00:01:25.322514Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-bdd5","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-e220","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-07-19T00:01:25.47268Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Updated link_ctx.c with NVS-based tuple persistence for EUI/keys/replay-counters/epoch/tx_seq/nonce using statics + CRC (existing nvs_persistence + meshcore pattern). Updated init() for restore on boot, load_key() for save after key load. No dynamic alloc, C11. Reboot simulation via NVS remount in tests passes. No dead code. Conventional commit prepared.
